idea-plugin.name: SlopGuard [formerly EntropyGuard]
idea-plugin.description:
SlopGuard [formerly EntropyGuard] — AI Code Quality Protection

AI-generated code is silently degrading your codebase. SlopGuard [formerly EntropyGuard] catches the hidden quality issues that compound over time—security vulnerabilities, duplicate boilerplate, and structural decay introduced by AI assistants.

The Problem

AI-generated code isn't broken—it's slowly rotting your codebase. Each AI-generated commit adds hidden technical debt: hardcoded secrets, repetitive boilerplate, and anti-patterns that don't fail immediately but make your code harder to maintain, debug, and extend over time.

What SlopGuard [formerly EntropyGuard] Catches

Security Risks (HIGH Priority)

Detects dangerous patterns commonly introduced by AI assistants:

  • Hardcoded API tokens (AWS, GitHub, Google, Stripe, JWT)
  • Placeholder domains in production code (example.com, localhost)
  • Plaintext passwords and credentials
  • Unsafe null assertions (!!) and empty catch blocks
  • Broad exception handling that masks errors

Code Duplication

Identifies repetitive AI-generated boilerplate that bloats your codebase:

  • Duplicate string and number literals across files
  • Repeated method calls and similar logic patterns
  • Excessive getter/setter boilerplate
  • Large method signatures with too many parameters

Complexity Issues

Flags overly complex code that becomes unmaintainable:

  • Methods longer than 50 lines
  • Deep nesting beyond 3 levels
  • Complex boolean logic and long if/else chains
  • Excessive null checks and defensive programming

Malformed Code

Detects when AI generates non-code content:

  • Markdown artifacts and code fences left in source files
  • Unbalanced braces and syntax errors
  • Mixed language content (prose mixed with code)

Key Benefits

  • Real-time Protection: Analyze code changes as you type, catching issues before they reach your repository
  • Quality Scoring: Get instant risk scores (0-100) with prioritized findings
  • Inline Feedback: See issues highlighted directly in your editor
  • Comprehensive Coverage: Java (full AST analysis), Kotlin & Scala (duplicate detection & corrupted source detection)
  • Team Consistency: Enforce quality standards across AI-assisted development

Example Issues Detected

SlopGuard [formerly EntropyGuard] catches problems like:

  • String apiKey = "sk-1234567890abcdef"; — Hardcoded secrets
  • url = "https://api.example.com/v1"; — Placeholder domains
  • 20 nearly identical validation methods across controllers
  • Classes with 40+ getter/setter methods (boilerplate bloat)
  • Markdown code fences accidentally pasted into Java files

How It Works

SlopGuard [formerly EntropyGuard] uses structural pattern detection to identify AI-generated anti-patterns. It combines AST analysis for Java with fuzzy matching across languages, catching issues that traditional linters miss.

Getting Started

  1. Install: Install from JetBrains Marketplace
  2. Code: Write code as usual—AI-assisted or manual
  3. Review: Check the SlopGuard [formerly EntropyGuard] tool window for findings
  4. Fix: Click issues to jump to source and resolve them

Supported Technologies

  • Languages: Java (full AST analysis), Kotlin & Scala (duplicate detection & corrupted source detection)
  • Frameworks: Spring, React, Angular, Vue.js, Django, Flask
  • IDE: IntelliJ IDEA 2023.3+

Licensing

  • Free Trial: 7 days to evaluate

action.description: Run AI code risk analysis on the current file
Run comprehensive AI risk analysis on the entire project

idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.1.2 - 2026-05-16
  • Added Scala language support via tree-sitter grammar for fuzzy duplicate detection and pattern-based analysis
  • Fixed Kotlin parser support - Kotlin files now correctly use Kotlin parser instead of Java parser
  • Added Kotlin and Scala parsers to CorruptedSourceDetector for corrupted source detection
  • Implemented Scala-specific analysis patterns: pattern matching complexity, implicit usage detection, for-comprehension nesting, case class boilerplate, unsafe Option.get usage
  • Added comprehensive test coverage for both Kotlin and Scala language support
1.1.1 - 2026-05-14
  • Renamed plugin to SlopGuard [formerly EntropyGuard]
  • Updated marketing copy to better address AI-generated code quality issues
  • Improved description clarity with concrete examples of detected issues
1.1.0 - 2026-04-30
  • Renamed plugin to EntropyGuard
  • Added detection of corrupted source files as a new top-level category
  • Improved detection of common AI duplication signals
  • Improved detection of common AI security anti-patterns
  • Got code generator tool 100% working
1.0.3 - 2026-04-25
  • Added new improved icon
  • Fixed licensing to correctly use IntelliJ Marketplace Trial API
  • Fixed code generator compilation issues
  • Added support for aichat, llm, and ollama CLI tools
1.0.2 - 2026-04-17
  • Disabled MCP server (temporarily)
  • Click to jump to problem files
  • UI layout improvements
1.0.1 - 2026-04-14
  • General improvements and bug fixes
1.0.0 - 2026-04-12
  • Initial release to IntelliJ Marketplace
  • Real-time code risk analysis
  • Security vulnerability detection
  • Code quality insights and suggestions
  • Inline editor feedback
  • Dedicated tool window for analysis results
